strengths: 19+ years of hands-on engineering across web, mobile, embedded, and high-throughput backend systems (millions of HTTP req/s at Dunnhumby)
Founder experience: built and operates GISCollective, an open-source geo-data platform running on Kubernetes, used by Green Map
Current AI-adjacent work at re:cinq on MCP code generators and agentic-workflow infrastructure, plus a record of public talks and workshops
concerns: No principal/director-level scope: career history is senior/staff IC delivery roles, with no evidence of org-wide technical strategy or influencing product direction at scale
Limited AI/ML depth: AI exposure begins in 2025 with agent tooling; no ML systems, no AI-powered products at scale, no recognized AI expertise or publications
No advanced degree in an AI-related field and no evidence of advising strategic customers/partners on cloud or AI adoption

key_factors.factor: Recognized AI expertise and AI-built products

response.justification: The role's core competencies are AI strategy leadership, recognized AI expertise with shipped AI products, cloud platform vision, and executive-level influence. The candidate clears the minimum bar: a BS in Computer Science (MIT) and roughly 19 years of engineering experience spanning low-latency DLang microservices, TypeScript/AWS product work, and Kubernetes deployments. Entrepreneurial signal exists via founding GISCollective and community teaching (FOSS4G talk, Selenium course). However, the preferred qualifications that define this Director-level IC role are largely absent: AI experience is limited to MCP tooling since April 2025, there is no advanced AI degree, no demonstrated AI products or platforms of note, and no evidence of setting technical strategy, advising strategic customers, or operating beyond senior/staff delivery scope. Strong senior engineer, but the evidence does not support a principal AI-strategy hire.
